response:
Robert M. Fomon was a financier who served as Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of E. F. Hutton & Co., and was a governor of the New York Stock Exchange. He was also Chairman of the Board of Governors of the Pacific Stock Exchange. 

He was not directly associated with the physical location of the New York Stock Exchange at 11 Wall Street, but he held significant influence and leadership roles within the exchange's governance structure.
